div.p: sizeof(SHA256_CTX) = 112 bytes · No heap allocation · Thread-safe for distinct contexts
SHA256_INITIAL_HASH[8] extern const uint32_t frac(√prime) × 2³² H[0]=0x6a09e667 … H[7]=0x5be0cd19
void sha256_compress( uint32_t H[8], const uint8_t block[64]) 64-round compression · updates H in-place Davies-Meyer feed-forward: H[i] += working_var[i]
void sha256_message_schedule( const uint8_t *block, uint32_t W[64]) Parse 16 BE words · expand 48 via σ0/σ1 recurrence
Single-call API sha256_oneshot(msg, len, hex) ↳ stack-local ctx → thread-safe ↳ calls init + update + finalize + to_hex

Multi-call API sha256_init(ctx) sha256_update(ctx, chunk₁, n₁) sha256_update(ctx, chunk₂, n₂) ← N calls, any sizes … sha256_finalize(ctx, digest) sha256_to_hex(digest, hex) ⚠ Must reinit between computations
void sha256_init(SHA256_CTX *ctx) Loads SHA256_INITIAL_HASH → H[0..7] Sets buf_len = 0, total_bits = 0 Must call before first update() and before reuse

void sha256_update(SHA256_CTX *ctx, const uint8_t *data, size_t len) Phase 1 Fill existing partial buf → flush if full Phase 2 Compress whole blocks directly from data Phase 3 Buffer remainder (0–63 bytes) total_bits += (uint64_t)len × 8

void sha256_finalize(SHA256_CTX *ctx, uint8_t digest[32]) Append 0x80 · zero-fill · write 64-bit BE length if buf_len+1 > 56 → extra block (two compressions) Serialize H[0..7] → digest via explicit byte-shift ctx is indeterminate after return; reinit before reuse
void sha256_to_hex(const uint8_t digest[32], char hex[65]) Each byte → 2 lowercase hex chars via nibble extraction hex[64] = '\0' · table: "0123456789abcdef" Returns void · no allocation
void sha256_oneshot(const uint8_t *msg, size_t len, char hex[65]) Stack-local SHA256_CTX · init → update → finalize → to_hex Thread-safe · no shared state · no heap
